signals.headline: No resolution or formal ceasefire announced; escalation via blockade overshadows talks, unlikely to move prediction mark
signals.raw: No resolution or formal ceasefire announced; escalation via blockade overshadows talks, unlikely to move prediction markets toward "yes" on ceasefire[1][2].

signals.headline: **Blockade Announcement**: President Trump has enacted a US naval blockade on Iran's ports and coastal areas, effective
signals.raw: **Blockade Announcement**: President Trump has enacted a US naval blockade on Iran's ports and coastal areas, effective as of April 14, 2026, declaring any Iranian fast-attack ships approaching US vessels will be "immediately eliminated." Tehran vows retaliation[1].

signals.headline: South Korea March 2026 Inflation Rises to 2
signals.raw:
South Korea March 2026 Inflation Rises to 2.2%, Below Forecasts | NO | LOW confidence**  
South Korea's annual inflation rate increased to 2.2% in March 2026 from 2.0% prior, missing market expectations of 2.4%; this is the most recent data but not "right now" as of April 15 and unlikely to shift prediction markets given its alignment with the central bank's 2% target and prior contained pressures
matched_markets: Will South Korea’s 2026 Annual Inflation be between 2.7% and 2.9%?

signals.headline: Germany January 2026 Inflation at 2
signals.raw:
Germany January 2026 Inflation at 2.1%, Above Prior Month | NO | LOW confidence**  
German headline inflation rebounded to 2.1% YoY in January 2026 from 1.8% in December, with core at 2.5%; dated over two months ago with no new confirmation, and forecasts suggest fluctuation around 2% without major market-moving impact.[2]

signals.headline: South Korea Q4 2025 GDP Contracts on Weak Exports/Investment Amid Oil Shock | NO | MED confidence**
signals.raw:
South Korea Q4 2025 GDP Contracts on Weak Exports/Investment Amid Oil Shock | NO | MED confidence**  
South Korea's GDP contracted in Q4 2025 (final quarter last year) at a slightly slower pace than estimated, due to weaker investment/exports and oil disruptions; reported March 10, 2026, this tempers growth outlook (projected 1.0% in 2025) but lacks fresh resolution to move markets now.[3][5]

signals.headline: **Ceasefire Developments**: Sources indicate Iran reached out to the US, with Trump telling the New York Post that talks
signals.raw: **Ceasefire Developments**: Sources indicate Iran reached out to the US, with Trump telling the New York Post that talks could occur within two days; this optimism is driving market reactions, though decisions remain fluid and no agreement is confirmed[2].
